title: cuscuta scabrella
intro: Cuscuta scabrella, also known as dodder, is a parasitic flowering plant that grows by attaching itself to the stems of other plants to obtain nutrients. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be utilized to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion, as well as its potential to treat respiratory issues and skin infections. Cuscuta scabrella can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional medicine to enhance its therapeutic effects.
